title: Volume formula intuition
description: Practice problems that help you see why the volume formula makes sense.
topic_path: Module 5: Addition and multiplication with volume and area

assessment_items.question_content:
The rectangular prism has [[☃ input-number 1]] layers separated by color.

Each colored layer is made up of [[☃ input-number 2]] unit cubes. 

**What is the volume of the rectangular prism?** [[☃ input-number 3]] cubic units

![A rectangular prism is divided into cubes.  The prism is 2 cubes deep and 4 cubes wide. The bottom layer is shaded red. The middle layer is shaded orange. The top layer is shaded blue. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0dcbc56a3612468d401870b64d8ce665008ac583)
hints:
The rectangular prism has $\pink3$ layers separated by color.
[[☃ image 1]]

We can count that the top layer is made up of $\blue8$ unit cubes.    
![The top layer is 2 cubes deep and 4 cubes wide. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bd2d86c79509b89f5a109ad379e923a4.png)

There are $\blue{8}$ cubes on each layer of the prism, and there are $\pink3$ layers, so we can find the total number of cubes by multiplying.  
  
$\blue{8} \times \pink3 = \green{24}$

The rectangular prism has $\pink3$ layers separated by color.

Each colored layer is made up of $\blue8$ unit cubes.

The volume of the rectangular prism is $\green{24}$ unit cubes.

There are $\blue{15}$ cubes on each layer of the prism, and there are $\pink2$ layers, so we can find the total number of cubes by multiplying.  
  
$\blue{15} \times \pink2 = \green{30}$

The rectangular prism has $\pink2$ layers separated by color.

Each colored layer is made up of $\blue{15}$ unit cubes.

The volume of the rectangular prism is $\green{30}$ unit cubes.
